item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: Tax Day
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: Jared Kushner’s Mysterious Role in the Trump Administration
item.description: Is the president’s son-in-law carrying out the public’s business or pursuing his own private interests?
item.dc:creator: Andrea Bernstein

item.title: A New Kind of Hybrid Car Is About to Hit America’s Streets
item.description: The car industry says it has an answer for drivers wary of going electric.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Moseman

item.title: The Paradox of Modern Medicine
item.description: Testing has become so advanced that doctors now miss important elements of diagnosis.
item.dc:creator: Meghan O’Rourke

item.title: The High-Risk, Low-Reward Blockade of Hormuz
item.description: Americans may not have the stamina for the economic pain and military losses ahead.
item.dc:creator: Brynn Tannehill

item.title: We Shouldn’t Need Accountants
item.description: America’s insane tax-filing process
item.dc:creator: Annie Lowrey

item.title: What Viktor Orbán’s Opponents Sacrificed to Beat Him
item.description: Hungary offers lessons in defeating right-wing populists.
item.dc:creator: Idrees Kahloon

item.title: Trump’s Logic for Blockading the Blockaders
item.description: He wants to use economic pain to weaken Iran—even if that threatens the global economy.
item.dc:creator: Will Gottsegen

item.title: A Blasphemous President
item.description: Bullying won’t work against a power that has little need to curry favor.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Bruenig

item.title: AtlanticTrivia for April 14, 2026: Scripture and Special Ops
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: The Guitar Sounds New Again
item.description: The grungy, extraterrestrial “Mk.gee tone” is everywhere and depends on a decades-old device.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Walecki

item.title: SpaceX Is Basically a Huge Meme Stock
item.description: The company may be losing money, but it will soon be the most expensive big stock in the market.
item.dc:creator: James Surowiecki

item.title: Trump’s Latest Meltdown
item.description: Attacking the pope was only part of the president’s disturbing night on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Tom Nichols

item.title: What Does Lena Dunham Want to Tell Us?
item.description: Her new memoir captures the cost of being an impossibly popular target.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Gilbert

item.title: The Strange Origin of AI’s ‘Reasoning’ Abilities
item.description: It involves 4chan, of all places.
item.dc:creator: Alex Reisner

item.title: The 10 Things the Bezoses Are Almost Certainly Grateful for Each Morning
item.description: Never mind. We said “money” yesterday.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Petri

item.title: The Eighth Deadly Sin
item.description: Humankind has devised a new form of debasement.
item.dc:creator: James Parker

item.title: Don’t Just Replace Chavez—Rethink Monuments
item.description: It’s time for tributes to leave the great-man theory of history behind.
item.dc:creator: Carolina A. Miranda

item.title: The Challenge the Artemis II Crew Gave the Rest of Us
item.description: Hold on to “moon joy.”
item.dc:creator: Lynn Sherr

item.title: Trump Ditched Hearts and Minds in the Iran War
item.description: The military may need to cure its counterinsurgent “hangover,” but the president has the wrong solution.
item.dc:creator: Missy Ryan
